questions.prompt: The primary users of financial accounting reports are:
choices: Investors and employees
Investors and lenders
Employees and department managers
Investors and customers
questions.explanation: Financial accounting is designed for EXTERNAL users — primarily investors (who decide whether to invest) and lenders (who decide whether to extend credit). Managerial accounting serves internal users like managers and employees.

questions.prompt: Which of the following is NOT one of the three sources of the regulatory framework for financial reporting in Singapore?
choices: Companies Act
Accounting Standards (SFRS/IFRS)
Stock exchange regulations
Board of Directors' internal policies
questions.explanation: The three regulatory sources are: (1) Companies Act — sets legal obligations for directors; (2) Accounting Standards (SFRS/IFRS) — detailed rules for recording transactions; (3) Stock Exchange regulations — for listed companies. Internal board policies are not part of the external regulatory framework.

choices: Assets are recorded at liquidation (fire-sale) prices
The business is assumed to continue operating indefinitely, so assets are valued at cost
All liabilities must be paid within one year
Financial statements must be produced annually
questions.explanation: Going-concern assumes the business will continue operating. This means assets are NOT valued at what they'd fetch in a quick liquidation sale — they are valued based on their ongoing use. Only if a business is about to close would liquidation values be used.

questions.prompt: A consulting firm completes a $10,000 project in December but receives payment in January. Under accrual accounting, in which month is the revenue recognised?
choices: January — when cash is received
December — when the service is performed
Split evenly between December and January
Neither — it is only recognised when the invoice is issued
questions.explanation: Under the accrual basis, revenue is recognised when EARNED — when the service is performed (December), not when cash is received (January). This ensures revenue is matched to the correct accounting period.

questions.prompt: Inventory was purchased for $10,000 but can now only be sold for $8,000 due to damage. Under the Conservatism principle, the inventory should be recorded at:
choices: $10,000 — always use historical cost
$9,000 — the average of cost and market value
$8,000 — the lower of cost or market value
$0 — damaged goods have no accounting value
questions.explanation: Conservatism (Prudence) requires recording assets at the LOWER of cost or market value. If market value ($8,000) falls below cost ($10,000), the inventory must be written down. This prevents overstating assets.

questions.prompt: Unearned Revenue is best classified as:
choices: Asset — it represents future cash to be received
Revenue — the company has received the money
Liability — the company owes a service to the customer
Equity — the customer has invested in the company
questions.explanation: Unearned Revenue is a LIABILITY. The company received cash but has NOT yet performed the service. It owes the customer a future service — this is an obligation. Once the service is performed, Unearned Revenue (Liability) decreases and Revenue increases.

questions.prompt: Which of the following is a FLOW account?
choices: Accounts Receivable
Bank (Cash)
Salary Expense
Share Capital
questions.explanation: Salary Expense is a FLOW account — it is measured over a period of time (e.g., salary expense for the year) and resets to zero at the start of each accounting period. It appears on the Income Statement. Accounts Receivable, Bank, and Share Capital are STOCK accounts (measured at a point in time, on the Balance Sheet).

questions.prompt: A company's Balance Sheet shows: Cash $22,000, Accounts Receivable $16,000, Equipment $50,000, and Accounts Payable $17,000. What is Owner's Equity?
questions.explanation: Assets = $22,000 + $16,000 + $50,000 = $88,000. Liabilities = $17,000. Equity = Assets − Liabilities = $88,000 − $17,000 = $71,000. This applies the fundamental equation: Assets = Liabilities + Equity.

questions.prompt: Which financial statement shows whether the company earned a profit during the period by listing revenues and expenses?
choices: Statement of Financial Position (Balance Sheet)
Statement of Cash Flows
Income Statement (Profit or Loss)
Statement of Changes in Equity
questions.explanation: The Income Statement (also called Statement of Profit or Loss) lists all revenues earned and expenses incurred during a period. Revenue − Expenses = Net Profit or Net Loss. It is a FLOW statement covering a period (e.g., one year).

questions.prompt: A company purchases a new factory machine for $80,000 cash. On the Statement of Cash Flows, this is classified as:
choices: Operating activity — it is part of running the business
Investing activity — it is the purchase of a long-term asset
Financing activity — it involves spending cash
It does not appear on the Cash Flow Statement
questions.explanation: Buying a long-term asset (factory machine) is an INVESTING activity on the Cash Flow Statement. Operating activities relate to day-to-day business operations (sales, salaries, utilities). Financing activities relate to borrowing, issuing shares, or paying dividends.
